_meta.note: LOCAL VA Tidewater batch 2026-06-04 18:30 — Virginia Beach core: Mayor Dyer + Council D2 Henley + Council D7 Jackson-Green + Commonwealth's Attorney Stolle, plus lue-ward confidence-promotion (Suffolk Vice Mayor — prior 17:50 batch exhaustively swept; promoting roster_verified -> evidence_local on the cited single self_defense q1 TRUE so he exits the picker churn per the documented two-sweep precedent). rick-west DEFERRED 9TH-consecutive (Chesapeake Mayor — slug-collision with OK State Rep Rick West remains; refine-records.py:366 by_slug last-wins; OK record at index 5439 silently overwrites VA record at index 497; per runbook, NOT re-escalating warning-level Adam notification — 7th-defer ping already sent). HETERODOX BATCH: 4 of 5 records here are heterodox-direction catches: (a) Henley = 12th-gen rural farmer who voted NO on 2A Constitutional City (post-2019 mass-shooting context) AND sole NO on FY26 meals-tax-hike budget; (b) Dyer = R-affiliated Mayor with strong 2A/police/COVID-mask record but also issued June 2020 LGBT Pride Month proclamation + ousted HRC member over anti-gay comments + voted YES on FY26 meals-tax hike; (c) Jackson-Green = R-affiliated newcomer with HARD christian_liberty q0 (life-coach in churches per VB Police Foundation bio) but voted YES on FY26 tax-hike + frames youth violence in mental-health terms; (d) Stolle = long-tenure R prosecutor with TOTAL silence on culture-war cells (pro-life, marriage, parental rights, 2A, ICE) BUT pursued public_justice cells densely with self-initiated investigation of his own former prosecutor for embezzling victim funds + prosecuted Rep. Scott Taylor 2018 petition fraud. CORRECTION TO BRIEFING: Stolle's father is Ed Stolle (Navy 22 yrs), NOT a state senator. The Stolle political dynasty is sibling-level: brother Ken (state Sen 1992-2010, VB Sheriff 2010-2023); brother Chris (former Delegate); sister Sen. Siobhan Dunnavant.

profile.background: Mayor of City of Virginia Beach (officially nonpartisan; R-affiliated/Republican-endorsed; Youngkin endorsed his 2024 re-election). Born ~1949 (~76-77 yo). USMC veteran 1968-72. Physical therapist 40+ yrs in homecare. PhD organizational leadership, Regent University 2001; former asst professor of government at Regent. Won Centerville District Council seat 2004 (14 yrs pre-Mayor service); sworn in as Mayor Nov 20 2018 in special election after Will Sessoms resigned. Re-elected Nov 5 2024 with 40.9% in 5-way race (vs Wooten/Moss/Taylor/Kowalewitch); current 4-year term ends Dec 31 2028. Christian faith publicly professed in Regent alumni interview ('the good Lord had his hand on my shoulder'); specific denomination/home church not confirmed in sources reviewed.

bobby-dyer.notes_set: [2026-06-04 18:30 RESOLUTE refinement] Dense local record after 7+ years as Mayor + 14 yrs as Centerville Councilman. 30 LOCAL cells scored as 7T / 4F / 19 NULL = 64% (11 scored cells). HARD TRUE christian_liberty q0 — Regent alumni interview faith-profession in office: 'I honestly felt all along that the good Lord had his hand on my shoulder'; 'you can't do it by yourself … so I pray'; 'I really think that the big answer to a lot of social ills is going to be grounded in faith.' SOFT TRUE christian_liberty q3 — keynote at Regent Veterans Prayer Breakfast + post-2019 mass shooting call for prayer / framing event as 'an act of evil.' HARD TRUE public_justice q0 — explicit 'I will never ever tell a police stand down' op-ed defense of police funding ('Defunding Beach police would imperil city'); FOP+PBA endorsements; created Mayor's Task Force for a Safer Virginia Beach. SOFT TRUE refuse_state_overreach q0 — repeatedly pressed Northam to relax COVID restrictions (July 23 2020 letter; Labor Day 2020 restaurant-restriction rollback request; pushed for Phase-1 beach opening). HARD TRUE refuse_state_overreach q2 — Aug 12 2021 Council meeting on the record: 'masks really don't give much protection' citing (later-retracted) studies; stood by comment after media criticism. SOFT TRUE border_immigration q1+q2 — May 2025 DHS sanctuary list pushback: city office explicitly rejected sanctuary status + Dyer campaign quote 'in Virginia Beach, where law and order matters, if someone is found to be illegally in the country, ICE can do what they're going to do'; VB has no sanctuary ordinance under his tenure. HARD TRUE self_defense q1 — Dyer CALLED the Jan 6 2020 special Council session and VOTED YES on the 6-4 Virginia Beach Second Amendment Constitutional City resolution (vs Henley/Jones/Rouse/Wooten NO) — explicitly pushing back on Richmond's then-pending red-flag/AWB/registry package, 7 months after the May 2019 Municipal Center mass shooting at his own City Hall complex; insisted on 'Constitutional City' NOT 'Sanctuary' language ('precedent and a slippery slope') — heterodox framing but still HARD YES on the affirmative 2A vote. HARD FALSES on biblical_marriage: q4 — Mayor's office issued June 2020 official LGBT Pride Month proclamation for VB; q2 — Oct 2019 publicly ousted Human Rights Commission member LaKendrick Coburn El over anti-gay/anti-trans Facebook posts ('no longer appropriate'); q0 SOFT FALSE — VB Mayor's office formally supports state-level LGBTQ nondiscrimination ('should not be legal to deny someone the opportunity to work, rent a home, or be served in public accommodation based on sexual orientation or gender identity'); all-gender single-stall restrooms in muni facilities. HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2 — voted YES in 10-1 majority on FY26 budget (May 13 2025) raising meals tax from 5.5% to 6%, raising stormwater + boat reg fees, cutting library positions (only Henley NO); FY25 held real-estate rate flat with ~6% assessment rise = effective tax hike. Heterodox catches noted in meta. Recommend revisit after any 2026 Pride proclamation update / school-board liaison action.

profile.background: Virginia Beach City Council, District 2 (Princess Anne / Pungo — rural southern VB) — longest-serving councilmember in modern VB history (Pungo Borough 1978-1990, Princess Anne 1994-2002 + 2006-present). Current term began Jan 1 2023 (Nov 8 2022 election under new post-VRA 10-district system); term ends Dec 31 2026 — UP IN NOV 2026. 12th-generation Pungo farmer (Henley Farms LP); former VBCPS elementary teacher; BS Elementary Education + MA Urban Studies Old Dominion University. Co-author with Susan Flanagan of published history of Charity United Methodist Church in Pungo — strong Methodist personal-faith proxy. Chief advocate for VB Agricultural Reserve Program (rural land protection). Officially nonpartisan; Progressive Voters Guide 2022 listed her (direction not verified — site 403); historically not a partisan label. No retirement announcement located.

barbara-henley.notes_set: [2026-06-04 18:30 RESOLUTE refinement] Big HETERODOX-RURAL-FARMER CATCH. 30 LOCAL cells scored as 2T / 1F / 27 NULL = 67%. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2 — sole 10-1 NO vote on FY26 budget (May 13 2025), explicitly objecting to the 0.5% meals-tax increase + questioning dedicating meals-tax revenue to public safety: 'I'm not sure why we want to dedicate the meal tax money to public safety, except to make it more acceptable to the people and say, Well, you're getting public safety if you pay this additional tax.' Cleanest economic-stewardship TRUE in this Tidewater sweep. SOFT TRUE refuse_state_overreach q0 — 40+ yr career chief advocate for VB Agricultural Reserve Program + rural-protection legislative wish-list items (subsidiarity-of-land-use pattern, soft because no single anti-state-mandate statement located). HARD FALSE self_defense q1 — voted NO on the Jan 6 2020 VB Second Amendment Constitutional City resolution (6-4 passed; Henley + Jones + Rouse + Wooten NO). The vote came 7 months after the May 2019 Municipal Center mass shooting at her own City Hall complex; rubric scores the vote, not the context. Counter-tribal break of the 12th-generation-Pungo-farmer archetype. NULL on christian_liberty q0 — Methodist-church-history co-authorship is personal/historical scholarship, NOT a public profession of Christ in capacity-of-office per discipline; flag as faith-adjacent but not q0-scoring. NULL on biblical_marriage q4 — VB issues annual Pride proclamations (2020+) but no individual Henley vote/statement on those proclamations located; insufficient under discipline. NULL on public_justice q0 — her FY26 NO vote included skepticism of public-safety framing (NOT anti-police but skeptical of how police funding is framed); does NOT meet HIGH BAR for affirmative advocacy. Recommend revisiting after Nov 2026 re-election + any future ARP-related local-control statement.

profile.background: Virginia Beach City Council, District 7 (officially nonpartisan; R-affiliated per RPVB elected-officials list rpvb.org/elected-officials; ran as Independent on nonpartisan ballot per Ballotpedia). Full name Calvern Franklin 'Cash' Jackson-Green Jr. Won Nov 5 2024 vs Mabinty Scott 53.32%/45.73%; sworn in Jan 1 2025; term ends Dec 31 2028. Prior occupation: 20+ yrs automotive sales (customer retention); life-coach facilitator (faith-based sessions in churches and care facilities). Married, father of 5 (3 graduated VB Public Schools, 2 in U.S. military). Civic boards: Virginia Beach Police Foundation (private 501(c)(3) supporting VBPD), VB Library Board (Council-appointed), Process Improvement Steering Committee, Glenwood Civic Assoc., VB Council of Civic Orgs (VBCC), Indian Lakes Assoc., 200+ Men Org., VFW Auxiliary, Rotary. Faith: VB Police Foundation bio states he 'deeply values Christianity, family, community service' and has served as a life-coach facilitator in churches — capacity-of-office public Christian witness.

cal-jackson-green.notes_set: [2026-06-04 18:30 RESOLUTE refinement] 30 LOCAL cells scored as 2T / 2F / 26 NULL = 50% (4 scored cells). HARD TRUE christian_liberty q0 — Virginia Beach Police Foundation board-member bio documents that he 'deeply values Christianity, family, community service' and has 'served as a life coach and facilitated sessions for churches' — public Christian witness explicitly in capacity-of-office (board-member bio published by the foundation he serves as City Councilman). HARD TRUE public_justice q0 — board-member of Virginia Beach Police Foundation, a private 501(c)(3) explicitly supporting VBPD beyond city budget — affirmative pro-LE advocacy/program meeting HIGH BAR (not merely a budget vote; an outside-board commitment to police support). HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2 — voted YES with 10-1 majority on FY26 budget (May 13 2025) raising meals tax 5.5%->6%, boat-reg fee hikes, library cuts (only Henley NO). SOFT FALSE public_justice q3 — campaign platform framed youth gun violence in 'mental health' terms ('addressing youth gun violence and mental health' per WAVY candidate profile + 13NewsNow community-meeting coverage) — public-health framing the rubric flags. NULL elsewhere — no statements on abortion, marriage / SOGI, school choice, parental notification, ICE cooperation, 2A (VB's 2A Constitutional City resolution predates his tenure Jan 2025), election integrity. Heterodox catches: (a) R-affiliated councilman voted YES on FY26 meals-tax hike — counter-tribal economic FALSE; (b) HARD christian_liberty TRUE via on-record church-facilitation faith witness; (c) HARD public_justice q0 TRUE via outside-board commitment to police, not just budget vote; (d) mental-health framing of youth violence counterweights the police-foundation board service. SLUG NOTE (advisory only — not changed in this dossier): public-facing name is 'Cash Jackson-Green'; slug 'cal-jackson-green' is technically correct (Cal = legal first name Calvern) but Adam may want to add a 'cash-jackson-green' alias on next infra pass for searchability. Recommend revisit after any Library Board content-restriction vote (he sits on it).

profile.background: Virginia Beach Commonwealth's Attorney (R). Full name Colin Dwyer Stolle. Born ~1970 (family moved to VB 1971); ~55 yo. Asst CA from 1996, Deputy CA from 2004, first elected CA Nov 2013 (took office Jan 2014). Re-elected Nov 4 2025 with 52% over D Suzanne Seidel Richmond (her first opponent in 12 years); new 4-year term Jan 2026 - Dec 2029. Star of the Sea Catholic School elementary + Cape Henry Collegiate prep (Catholic upbringing — soft inference only, not a public profession). Wife Denise; sons Colin Jr. and Ian. Family lineage CORRECTION: father is Ed Stolle (USN 22 yrs), NOT a state senator. The Stolle political dynasty is sibling-level — brother Ken Stolle (state Sen 1992-2010, VB Sheriff 2010-2023, retired); brother Chris Stolle (former Delegate); sister Sen. Siobhan Dunnavant. 2021 Robert F. Horan Award (VA Assoc of Commonwealth's Attorneys); 2024 appointment by Gov. Youngkin to Virginia State Crime Commission; NDAA board member.

colin-stolle.notes_set: [2026-06-04 18:30 RESOLUTE refinement] STANDOUT — 12-year R prosecutor with TOTAL public-record silence on culture-war cells but DENSE prosecutor-portfolio TRUEs. 30 LOCAL cells scored as 4T / 1F / 25 NULL = 80% (5 scored cells, highest density in this batch). HARD TRUE election_integrity q1 — investigated alleged election-law violations by U.S. Rep. Scott Taylor's 2018 re-election campaign (signatures on nominating petitions) + requested Special Prosecutor; grand jury indicted School Board candidate Burns (Sept 2020) on alleged election-fraud / forged-document charges from his office's work — prosecutor's affirmative election-integrity portfolio. HARD TRUE public_justice q0 — campaign-site claim 'VB seeing some of the lowest crime rates in its history' attributed to 'partnership among VB residents, law enforcement, and the Office of the Commonwealth's Attorney'; jointly launched First Step Program with VBPD (any precinct, voluntary medical-detox 'I need help' model); dedicated firearms-offense prosecutor; publicly ruled 2020 Olivo officer-involved shooting JUSTIFIED — affirmative HIGH-BAR pro-LE prosecutor work. HARD TRUE public_justice q1 — on the record: 'My office does not agree to bond on any violent offense'; 'My office seeks active jail or prison time for any violent offense'; 'no deals for dangerous drug dealers'; 'use a gun, go to jail'; juveniles using weapons charged as adults. Endorsed by AG Miyares + Youngkin (Crime Commission appointment) + Lt. Gov. Sears + Sheriff Holcomb. Critic-source Filter Magazine calls him 'notoriously draconian' — adversarial confirmation of NOT-progressive-prosecutor posture. HARD TRUE public_justice q2 — self-initiated criminal investigation of his OWN former prosecutor (James Panagis) for embezzling victim-restitution funds; immediately requested VA State Police + Special Prosecutor; emphasizes victim sentencing input ('convicted criminals should not have more control over the sentencing process than the victim'); Criminal Injuries Compensation Fund + victim notification per VB official bio. SOFT FALSE public_justice q3 — explicitly embraces public-health framing for SUBSTANCE ABUSE ('the link between mental health, substance abuse and criminal behavior is one of the most important issues we face'); runs Drug Treatment Court, Behavioral Health Docket, First Step (voluntary diversion to medical detox), Narcan distribution via asset-forfeiture funds. Pairs it with tough-on-violent-crime posture but rubric-flag framing IS present. NULL EVERYWHERE ELSE — striking total silence on pro-life, marriage, parental rights, 2A, ICE cooperation, sanctuary across 30 years of public service. Sibling political ecosystem: brother Ken (now-retired Sheriff who cooperated heavily with ICE — 78 inmate transfers, May 2025 DHS list pushback) is family network but Colin himself has NOT made the ICE statements his brother did (per discipline: do NOT score by family inference). CRITICAL CORRECTION TO PRIOR-SESSION NOTES: father is Ed Stolle (Navy 22yrs), NOT a state senator named 'Ken Sr.' — that confusion swaps father and older brother. Heterodox catches: (a) zero faith profession despite Catholic-school upbringing — atypical for R prosecutor in red coastal city, stays purely secular; (b) public-health framing on substance abuse is explicit q3 FALSE — counterweight to tough-on-violent-crime branding; (c) self-investigated own former prosecutor for embezzlement — strong integrity signal; (d) prosecuted Rep. Scott Taylor's 2018 petition fraud — willingness to take on intra-party Republican target.

set.office: Vice Mayor / City Council - Nansemond Borough

profile.background: Suffolk City Council (officially nonpartisan; ran as Independent on 2012 ballot), Nansemond Borough since Jan 2, 2013 (4 consecutive terms — repeatedly unopposed; Nov 2024 won ~98% over write-ins). Vice Mayor since Jan 3, 2023 (elected by colleagues). Term ends Dec 31, 2028; NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. Founder/publisher Harambee Communications: Suffolk Happenings newspaper, Hampton Roads Happenings newspaper, Having Church Magazine (the magazine title is a Black-church cultural marker but not a personal profession). Hosts 30-min TV and radio show. Former president Nansemond-Suffolk NAACP branch. Sits on Western Tidewater Regional Jail Authority Board + City/School Advisory Committee on Collaborative Fiscal Concerns. Suffolk Democratic Committee does NOT list him.

Per runbook STEP 4, an exhaustively-swept official with one cited TRUE qualifies for evidence_local (the prior batch's 'roster_verified' classification was overly conservative and re-surfaced him in the picker pool — promoting per the documented two-sweep precedent so he honestly exits the picker queue). 30 LOCAL cells scored as 1T / 0F / 29 NULL = 100% (sparse-data, single-cell denominator). Johnson absent), which named and opposed the then-pending Virginia red-flag/magazine-limit/AWB/registry package. NULL everywhere else per discipline: no first-person quotes / campaign Facebook / VPAP record / candidate Q&A surfaced in 2026-06-04 17:50 sweep. Recommend revisiting only on a documented new vote (Pride/sanctuary/abortion/firearms/CRT-SOGI) or 2028 re-election campaign launch.
